title: Question: Support packed structures problems
body:
---


Author Name: **Serj Berlyakov**
Original Redmine Message: 903 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hello! The problem is the program doesn't compile my code with Verilog structures. 
It was announced that Verilator supports packed structures since 3.840 so I updated the program up to last version.
For example I add this code to my .sv module:


typedef struct packed
{
	logic [7:0]			a;
	logic [7:0]			b;
} str;	

str				cat;


It produced  an error while verilating the project:


%Error: top.sv:63: Unsupported: SystemVerilog 2005 reserved word not implemented: struct

%Error: top.sv:63: Unsupported: SystemVerilog 2005 reserved word not implemented: packed


As it was in the previous version.
For the compilation in my make-file I use a string:

verilator -trace -trace-depth 4 -cc ./top.sv <...> - like this.

Maybe I do something wrong? 
I work on Ubuntu 11.10 Kernel 3.0.0-17-generic -- if it's important information.


Thanks in advance.
